name: Breaded Eggplant Slices (Litton)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the eggplant into 1/2-inch slices. Halve any slices larger than 4 inches in diameter.
Yield: 14 to 16 slices.
2. Mix water and salt. Soak eggplant slices in the mixture for 5 minutes, then drain.
3. Blend oil and egg substitute in a shallow dish.
4. In another shallow dish, mix bread crumbs and wheat germ.
5. Dip each slice first in the egg mixture, then coat with the bread crumb mixture.
6. Arrange half of the coated slices around the edge of a microwave-safe baking sheet or platter lined with parchment paper.
7. Microwave at High for 8 to 11 minutes, rotating the baking sheet twice during cooking time, until just fork tender. Transfer to a wax paper-lined tray.
8. Repeat with the remaining slices.
9. Freeze in single layers until firm. Package in two freezer containers and label. Store no longer than 2 MONTHS.
Use in Eggplant Ricotta or Ratatouille Pie.
Adapted from Litton, Vol. 11: Microwave cooking from freezer (1982).
